Trickery and Corruption The vast majority of fortin­ cations-typica lly fortified towns­ that fell during the Middle Ages and early Modern period did so to tac­ tics other than standard siege war­ fare. In fact. almost as ma n y European cita dels were take n by bribing the caste llan or suborni ng the governor as by a n y other means. Ifbribe ry was ineffective or inap­ propriate. there we re many other optio ns still open. The most fa­ mous stratage m in history Is the Trojan hors

Desecration Most feudal soc ieties hold a great respec t for th e dead a nd the places in which they rest. Thus, desecra­ lion of a tom b or burial area (a pop­ ular pastime with many adventurers!)
